design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling jungle levels by collecting gems to unlock progression gates, then locate a story character or defeat a boss to advance; manage limited time (6 minutes per level) while avoiding/defeating enemies using projectile weapons and platforming
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (gems as progression gates) vs. time pressure (6-minute level limit); enemy avoidance vs. projectile-based combat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Guide Mowgli through the jungle as portrayed in Disney's film, encountering beloved characters (Baloo, Bagheera, King Louie, Kaa) while pursuing Shere Khan; wield jungle weapons and survival skills
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict gem-collection gating (8/10/12 of 15 required based on difficulty) forces thorough level exploration; combination of time limits with exploration-based progression creates tension between speed and completionism; boss encounters positioned every other level to punctuate narrative chapters

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three explicit difficulty tiers adjusting gem collection requirements: easy (8/15 gems), medium (10/15 gems), hard (12/15 gems); difficulty scales exploration demand rather than enemy patterns

progression.upgrade_system: Powerup collection during levels: invincibility masks, double banana shots, boomerang bananas; banana projectile as starting weapon
progression.unlock_mechanism: Gem collection gates: player must collect required gem count before accessing level exit character/boss; each chapter opens with story context description before stage begins
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty modes with different gem thresholds encourage replay at higher difficulties
Point-scoring system via gems and collectible fruits incentivizes optimization
Vine-swinging and platforming variety across jungle-themed levels

world_building_techniques: Character encounters: player must find specific characters (Baloo, Bagheera, King Louie, Kaa) within levels to progress narrative
Chapter framing: each stage preceded by story description contextualizing Mowgli's journey and current objective
Enemy/boss roster drawn from film: Shere Khan as final antagonist, jungle animals as encounters
Level theming: side-scrolling jungle environments with vines, platforms, and fauna reflecting film setting
Soundtrack integration: uses Disney film compositions (The Bare Necessities, I Wanna Be Like You, Colonel Hathi's March) to reinforce narrative atmosphere

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters positioned every other level as narrative punctuation; requires projectile-based damage (jumping on bosses ineffective); tied to Jungle Book characters and antagonists
notable_bosses: Shere Khan (final boss, climactic confrontation tied to main plot)

faction_indicators: Antagonist: Kaa (hypnotist snake, presented as obstacle)
Primary Threat: Shere Khan (tiger hunting Mowgli throughout)
danger_signals: Shere Khan's presence driving narrative urgency (Mowgli must reach human village to escape)
Boss encounters signal escalating threat level every other level
Jungle wildlife hazards requiring avoidance or projectile defense
environmental_storytelling: Jungle settings with vines, platforms, and fauna establish hostile/wild environment requiring survival skills
Character placement within levels (Baloo in certain areas, etc.) creates encounters that advance plot
Progression from jungle interior toward human village represents Mowgli's narrative journey
